review_notes: In TM3 'DIWYGY' motif — classic microbial-opsin counterion position. D->E is conservative (charge-preserving, +1 carbon); D->N removes the carboxylate (expected disruptive or major spectral shift). If this is the real counterion, D->N likely abolishes pumping/cycle.
Adjacent to position 181 in the TM3 aromatic face. W->F removes indole NH; W->Y swaps aromatic for phenol. Both commonly used in spectral tuning studies.
In 'EITQYPHDP' motif. Y->F removes OH (tests H-bond role); Y->W enlarges aromatic. Position choice is softer — could easily be wrong; Y277 or Y284 are alternatives at similar heuristic value.
In classic TM6 'NDYAL' motif. If analog of BR D96 (Schiff-base proton donor), D->E may slow reprotonation; D->N should fully block it. Could instead be a chromophore-adjacent structural D.

homology_basis: identified by IGDAYL cyclase anchor reasoning + K context
review_notes: PROTECTED. Covalent Schiff base to retinal. Mutation abolishes chromophore.

homology_basis: IGDAYL motif at 499 = class III cyclase catalytic signature
review_notes: PROTECTED. Class III guanylyl cyclase active site + metal-coordinating residues. Range conservatively spans IGDAYL + 30 downstream positions to cover catalytic D500, D504 and likely substrate-specificity residues.
